title: AI Pass — Flow Body Workstream
subtitle: Card-level AI assistance in the living-doc flow view — Cmd+K palette, structured patch, diff-before-apply.
scope: Epic #82 workstream: patch schema, skills (Claude Code + Codex), server endpoints, registry extension, and compositor UI.
sourceCoverage: Epic #82, mockup at docs/living-doc-flow-ai-pass-mockup.html, and sub-tickets #83–#88.
objective: Ship a card-level AI pass into the flow body where a user selects a card, presses Cmd+K, picks an action, reviews a structured patch, and applies accepted changes — preserving the substrate's semantic stability by making every AI-proposed change declinable.
successCondition: A user can take a thin card (like an early proof-ladder rung) from placeholder to plan end-to-end via Cmd+K → Decompose → review diff → apply, using either Claude Code or Codex as the engine, without leaving the flow view.

objectiveFacets.name: Registry as guardrail
objectiveFacets.description: Every AI-proposed change is validated against its convergence-type contract before it reaches the user.

objectiveFacets.name: User guards intent
objectiveFacets.description: The diff is the review gate; each proposed change is individually declinable.

objectiveFacets.name: Semantic stability preserved
objectiveFacets.description: AI may not silently mutate governance structure — facets, coverage, invariants, and type boundaries stay trustworthy.

objectiveFacets.name: Cross-engine parity
objectiveFacets.description: Works with Claude Code and Codex alike; user picks the engine when both are installed.

objectiveFacets.name: Local only, no browser keys
objectiveFacets.description: No API keys in the HTML. Engine CLIs hold credentials; the local doc server is the bridge.

invariants.name: Every patch is validated before it reaches the UI
invariants.statement: The validator from #83 runs on every patch before any diff is rendered. A patch with contract violations is rejected or annotated, never silently applied.

invariants.name: Every proposed change is individually declinable
invariants.statement: The diff UI must expose a per-change checkbox. No bundle-apply without the ability to deselect individual changes.

invariants.name: No engine credentials live in the browser
invariants.statement: All engine invocations are shelled out by the local server to the CLI tool. The HTML never carries an API key or a provider token.

callouts.title: How to read this doc
items: Status Snapshot is the landing view — where the work is right now.
Architecture Decisions are settled calls. Challenge a card, don't create a new one.
Actions Inventory grows as type-specific actions get specified and shipped.
Components are revision-pinned anchors that flip from 'not-built' to 'current' as code lands.
Attempts tracks skill-prompt iterations once we start tuning per engine.

sections.title: Status Snapshot
sections.rationale: One scannable view of where the workstream is. Counts and one callout — not a copy of the epic's checklist.

callout.title: Current edge
items: Work starts with #83 (patch schema) — it gates every other piece.
#85 / #86 (skills) unlock once #83 lands; they can be built side-by-side.
#87 (server endpoints) needs #83 plus at least one skill; #88 (UI) is last.

sections.title: Architecture Decisions
sections.rationale: Five calls settled during the proposal conversation. Captured so the next session does not re-litigate; if an implementation challenges a decision, the card gets updated rather than duplicated.

data.name: Transport: extend the local doc server
notes: Endpoints /api/ai-pass/propose, /apply, /engines live on the existing server that already serves docs at localhost:4321. No separate daemon.
Rationale: engine credentials stay in the CLI. The browser never sees an API key, which matches the local-only principle.

data.name: Batch, not streaming, for v1
notes: Spinner during propose; render the completed patch when it arrives. Streaming inline edits would break the user-guards-intent contract — a partial patch is an invitation to premature apply.
Revisit for v2 if latency feels too opaque in practice.

data.name: Two separate skills, one per engine
notes: Claude Code and Codex each get their own skill file. Not a shared lowest-common-denominator prompt.
Tradeoff accepted: some duplication between skills, in exchange for provider-specific prompt tuning.

data.name: create-ticket changes apply without extra confirmation
notes: The overall Apply button gates the whole patch. A ticket-create change is unchecked like any other if the user wants to decline it in the diff.
Philosophy: people can always dive into the created ticket on GitHub and correct or close it. Avoid over-gating.

data.name: Engine preference persists server-side
notes: Shared across browser tabs and devices, matching the library manifest's persistence.
Written via PUT /api/ai-pass/engines/preferred; read via GET /api/ai-pass/engines.

sections.title: Actions Inventory
sections.rationale: The action set shown in the palette is a merged view of general actions (hardcoded) and type-specific actions (declared per convergence type in the registry via #84). This section is the canonical list; new convergence types declare their own aiActions there and appear here once documented.

data.name: Decompose into sub-cards (general)
notes: Umbrella card → N scoped sub-cards, new tickets via gh, coverage edges added. Mirrors the L4 proof-ladder decomposition done by hand in this session.

data.name: Enrich notes (general)
notes: Thin card → richer prose with callouts and references, matching the style of neighbour cards in the same section.

data.name: Verify against invariants (general)
notes: Check the card against applicable invariants; flag violations with the specific invariant id and quote the rule.

data.name: Propose coverage edges (general)
notes: Map the card to facets it carries. Surface orphan facets honestly — do not fabricate coverage.

data.name: Summarise (general)
notes: Concise one-paragraph summary of the card's current state. Read-only — never writes.

data.name: Type-specific action stubs (9 types)
notes: attempt-log: propose-supersession, find-shipping-commit.
code-anchor: check-revision-drift, propose-replacement-anchor.
symptom-observation: suggest-environment-variants, check-contradictions.
issue-orbit: refresh-github-state, reclassify-relationship.
maintainer-stance: check-evolution.
proof-ladder: check-monotonic-invariant.
decision-record: check-if-still-current.
capability-surface: propose-status-from-commits.
investigation-findings: check-still-holding.

sections.title: Components
sections.rationale: As the schema, validator, server endpoints, skills, and UI land, each gets a revision-pinned anchor here. Currently every card is marked changed-since-issue because the code has not yet been written; they flip to current when the respective sub-ticket closes.

data.name: Patch schema — living-doc-ai-patch/v1
data.what_it_does: JSON schema describing every change kind in the patch format emitted by skills and consumed by the server

why_it_matters.text: Every other component reads or writes this shape. If the schema churns, every downstream piece churns with it. Land first.

data.name: Patch validator
data.what_it_does: Validates a patch against the schema and against the target convergence-type contracts (required fields, allowed status values, orphan-facet detection)

why_it_matters.text: This is where semantic stability is enforced. If the validator is weak, AI can drift type boundaries silently and the substrate loses its guarantees.

data.name: Registry aiActions extension
data.what_it_does: Declares per-type AI actions so the palette can merge them with the general set

why_it_matters.text: New convergence types opt into AI help by declaring their own actions. No renderer, palette, or server changes required to add a new action to an existing type.

data.name: Server endpoints for AI pass
data.what_it_does: Shells out to engine CLI, validates the returned patch, applies accepted changes (including gh issue create for ticket-create changes), re-renders the doc

why_it_matters.text: The only component with authority to invoke gh and mutate JSON on disk. Must be airtight — it is the blast-radius boundary for AI-originated changes.

data.name: Skill: living-doc-ai-pass-claude
data.what_it_does: Claude-tuned skill that reads doc + registry + neighbourhood and emits a v1 patch on stdout

why_it_matters.text: Tuned for Claude's strengths: long-form prose, decomposition, structure preservation. Free to diverge from the Codex skill prompt.

data.name: Skill: living-doc-ai-pass-codex
data.what_it_does: Codex-tuned skill emitting the same v1 patch contract

why_it_matters.text: Tuned for Codex's strengths: terse, code-anchor-oriented checks, revision comparison. Free to diverge from the Claude skill prompt.

data.name: Cmd+K palette + diff sidebar UI
data.what_it_does: Interactive surface: Cmd+K opens the action palette, spinner during propose, diff slides in, per-change accept/decline, apply submits accepted changes

why_it_matters.text: The UX the user actually sees. Static mockup at docs/living-doc-flow-ai-pass-mockup.html is the spec.

sections.title: Attempts
sections.rationale: Records iterations on skill prompts, engine-specific behaviour differences, and probes that narrowed design decisions. Empty at workstream start; grows as we build and observe.
